page: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with a base clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it lacks integrated GPUs.

The AMD EPYC 9V45 processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 (with VNNI and BF16 support), and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. These hardware extensions facilitate efficient vector calculations and cryptographic operations.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core scaling presents a clear tradeoff due to the 2-vCPU limit; stress-ng multi-core performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the average tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak (bottom 25%). Memory bandwidth is moderate, with cached read-only small block speeds at 421.76 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 34.54 GB/sec.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, the F8-2ams_v7 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute power. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis (achieving 6.96 million SET operations per second in testing), and lightweight data processing. It is less suitable for multi-threaded compilation, heavy static web serving, or medium-to-large LLM inference, where its 2-vCPU constraint and lack of GPU acceleration limit throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine in the Famsv7 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 2 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor clocked at 4.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of memory per core. The server has no local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and contains no GPUs. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression ranking in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance is average, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak due to the 2-vCPU limit. This instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 64 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for caching and databases.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high memory-to-core density and top-tier single-threaded performance for caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs clocked at 4.5 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 32.0 GB of memory per core.
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded compression.
No local storage bundled, but supports Premium Storage.
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy, low-vCPU tasks.
